name: Rocky Road Brownies
recipeIngredient: 4 Squares Unsweetened Chocolate -- melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Grease a 9x13 inch pan generously with flour, tapping out excess.
3. In a large bowl, cream together sugar and butter until fluffy.
4. Add eggs one at a time, mixing until thick and lemon-colored, about 2 minutes.
5. Stir in melted chocolate, vanilla extract, cocoa powder, flour, and salt until combined.
6. Fold in milk chocolate chips and chopped walnuts.
7. Arrange marshmallows horizontally across the prepared pan with about 1 1/2 inch border around the edges.
8. Spoon batter over the marshmallows, hiding them as much as possible without covering the space between the marshmallows and the sides of the pan (the batter will spread during baking).
9. Spread remaining batter evenly in the center of the pan.
10. Bake until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out moist from melted chocolate but not from the batter, about 25 to 35 minutes.
11. Allow the brownies to cool completely on a rack before cutting into squares.
12. Transfer the cooled brownies to a serving platter using a wide metal spatula.
13. Wrap airtight and store in the refrigerator for up to 3 months. Serve chilled or at room temperature.
